input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
The most common cause is a **missing `BindingContext`**. Your page doesn't know which object to bind against.

**Fix — set the BindingContext**, either in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:YourViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

or in code-behind:

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new YourViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change after initial load.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank even though the ViewModel properties have values.

The most likely issue is that they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel. Without a `BindingContext`, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Another possible issue is that the ViewModel properties don't raise `PropertyChanged` notifications (i.e., don't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`), but that would only matter if the properties are set *after* the binding is established.


I should focus on the missing `BindingContext` as the primary culprit here, since that's the most straightforward explanation for why the bindings aren't working at all.
